question: Which countries have the highest percentage contribution from agriculture to their GDP, and how does this compare to their overall GDP values?

analysis.chart_type: Horizontal Bar Chart (Ordered by Agriculture Sector GDP % Contribution)
analysis.design_rationale: A horizontal bar chart allows clear ranking of countries by their agriculture sector's percentage contribution to GDP. Sorting bars from highest to lowest quickly highlights which countries are most agriculture-dependent. Overlaying country GDP values as text labels directly on bars provides quick comparison for how these high-agriculture countries rank economically, enhancing actionable insights in one glance.
analysis.target_insight: Visual ranking reveals top countries by agriculture sector GDP % contribution, and comparison with their overall GDP illuminates whether agriculture-driven economies tend to be lower or higher GDP countries.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: GDP (millions of $) value annotated on bar as text label; sorted descending

transformations: Convert df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] and df['GDP (millions of $)'] to numerical values, handling missing and non-numeric entries
Drop rows where 'Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution' is missing or not convertible to float
Sort DataFrame descending by 'Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'
Select top 15 countries for clear visualization
data_preparation_steps: df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], errors='coerce')
df_top = df.dropna(subset=['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'])
df_top = df_top.sort_values('Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', ascending=False).head(15)
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\1.csv")
df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], errors='coerce')
df['GDP (millions of $)'] = pd.to_numeric(df['GDP (millions of $)'].str.replace(',', ''), errors='coerce')
df_top = df.dropna(subset=['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'])
df_top = df_top.sort_values('Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution', ascending=False).head(15)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
bars = ax.barh(df_top['Country/Economy'], df_top['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], color="#4CAF50")
for i, (bar, gdp) in enumerate(zip(bars, df_top['GDP (millions of $)'])):
    ax.text(bar.get_width() + 0.6, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2, f"{gdp:,.0f}", va='center', ha='left', fontsize=10, color="#333")
ax.set_xlabel("Agriculture Sector GDP % Contribution")
ax.set_ylabel("Country/Economy")
plt.title("Top Countries by Agriculture Sector GDP % Contribution\nwith Overall GDP Value Labels")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
